title: Jeera Rice
description: A simple yet aromatic Indian rice dish prepared with fragrant basmati rice, cumin seeds, and whole spices. Perfect as an accompaniment to dals, curries, and kebabs.

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Fresh coriander leaves — a few sprigs, chopped (for garnish)

instruction.title: Step 1: Rinse the Rice
steps: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water 3-4 times until the water runs clear.
Soak the rice in water for 20-30 minutes, then drain.

instruction.title: Step 2: Heat the Ghee/Oil
steps: In a heavy-bottomed pan or a deep skillet, heat the ghee or oil over medium heat.

instruction.title: Step 3: Add Whole Spices
steps: Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves.
Sauté for about 30 seconds or until the cumin seeds start to crackle and the spices release their aroma.
Be careful not to burn them.

instruction.title: Step 4: Add the Rice
steps: Add the drained rice to the pan.
Gently sauté the rice in the spiced ghee for 2-3 minutes, stirring carefully to coat the grains and lightly toast them.

instruction.title: Step 5: Add Water and Salt
steps: Pour in 2 cups of water and add salt.
Increase the heat to bring it to a boil.

instruction.title: Step 6: Cook the Rice
steps: When the water starts boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pan with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for about 15 minutes or until the water is absorbed and the rice is cooked through.

instruction.title: Step 7: Rest
steps: Turn off the heat and let the rice rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
This allows the steam to finish cooking the rice evenly.

instruction.title: Step 8: Fluff and Garnish
steps: Remove the bay leaf and other whole spices if you prefer.
Fluff the rice gently with a fork.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve hot with dal or any Indian curry.
Pair with paneer, chicken, or vegetable dishes.
Garnish with fresh coriander before serving.

chefTips: Use aged basmati rice for the best fragrance and long grains.
Don't skip soaking the rice—it helps grains cook evenly and prevents breaking.
Ghee adds richness and authentic flavor but you can use oil for a lighter version.
You can customize whole spices based on your taste.
